name: baml
description: Teaches Claude Code to write idiomatic BAML (the baml_language rewrite). One example-dense skill framed around BAML's two halves: the statically-typed, expression-oriented LANGUAGE (basically TypeScript with snake_case methods and `name type` fields — types, classes, generics, closures, optional chaining, control flow, throws/catch/catch_all, stdlib) and the declarative LLM DSL that desugars into it (client<llm>, function + client:/prompt:, generator, test). Every example is compiler-verified against the baml_language CLI; covers how BAML differs from TypeScript, and defers to `baml describe` for everything else.
